assumptions: No Celonis account, team membership, invitation, open-signup link, SSO configuration, role, license seat, permissions, data source, data pool, Data Model, Studio package, or dashboard is assumed.
A browser, working business identity, and network connection are available.
No Free Plan registration, invitation acceptance, SSO or 2FA completion, team join, data connection, package creation, or View editing was performed during research.

developer_goal: Create or join Celonis Platform access from a no-account starting state, then reach the earliest Studio first-success outcome documented for application creation.

first_success.outcome: A Studio package is created with initial View content from an existing Data Model, opening the View layout editor.
first_success.observable_signal: The package is displayed and the View layout editing screen opens.

route_selection.surface: Celonis Platform self-service web onboarding into Studio
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Studio package with initial View from an existing Data Model
candidates.reason: Creating applications and analyzing data is the first developer-oriented task family in the general getting-started guide, and Creating packages documents View initial content plus the View layout editor terminal, but it requires a pre-existing Data Model and Studio access that a cold account may not have.

candidates.name: Blank or Automation Studio package without a Data Model
candidates.reason: Creating packages lists Blank and Automation initial content that do not require a Data Model, so they may be shorter Studio terminals than the View route but reach a different first-success artifact.

candidates.name: Free Plan registration at signup.celonis.com
candidates.reason: An official Free Plan registration URL exists, but the public page is client-rendered and does not expose required signup fields or the post-registration handoff into a licensed team with Studio and Data Model assets.

candidates.name: Administrator invitation or team open-signup join
candidates.reason: Official docs require every user to belong to a team and document invitation and open-signup paths, both of which depend on a prior administrator, privacy settings, seat capacity, and role assignment.

candidates.name: Data Integration first, then Studio
candidates.reason: Studio content creation assumes Data Models produced by Data Integration, while current release notes say platform Quickstart options were removed, so the cold-start data path is not a single documented shortcut.

candidates.name: Celonis Developer Center API route
candidates.reason: API getting started assumes an existing Celonis customer team and authentication method rather than defining a no-account Studio first success.

required_path.action: Obtain Celonis Platform team membership through Free Plan registration, an administrator invitation, or an administrator-enabled open-signup link, then authenticate with the team's configured identity method.

required_fields.label: Account or join fields required by the selected access route
required_fields.notes: Free Plan registration fields are not readable from the public signup page. Invitation and open-signup fields depend on team admin configuration and identity method.

required_path.observable_result: The developer is authenticated into a Celonis Platform team.

required_path.action: Open Studio in a space where package creation is permitted and an eligible Data Model is available for the selected View route.

required_fields.label: Existing Data Model
required_fields.notes: Required when initial package content is View or Analysis.

required_path.observable_result: Create package is available with the settings needed for the selected initial content.

required_path.action: Create a package with initial View content.

required_fields.label: Initial package content
required_fields.notes: View requires a Data Model before Create is allowed.

required_fields.label: Package key
required_fields.notes: May be auto-generated from the package name or edited before create.

required_path.observable_result: Celonis creates a package containing one View and one Knowledge Model.

required_path.action: Confirm the created package opens into the View layout editing screen.
required_path.observable_result: The package is displayed and the View layout editing screen is shown.

prerequisites.description: Belong to at least one Celonis Platform team.
Hold a license and Studio permissions that expose package creation for the selected task.
For the View package candidate, have access to an existing team Data Model produced by prior Data Integration work.

external_gates.description: Team administrators control invitations, open signup, roles, seat allocation, and sign-in method.
Default Celonis ID sign-in requires an email-delivered two-factor access token; SAML or OIDC moves authentication to an external identity provider.
Product features depend on the team's Celonis Platform license.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email delivery of Celonis ID two-factor tokens, invitation messages, or Free Plan activation mail when those routes are used.

platform_outcomes.description: Celonis creates the Studio package with the selected initial assets after Create.
For View initial content, Celonis opens the View layout editing screen after package creation.

excluded.reason: Documentation is evidence for the path, not a counted product action.

excluded.item: Team administration as the first-success terminal
excluded.reason: Admin & Settings success is an access or governance outcome, not Studio application creation.

excluded.item: Publishing, sharing, adding View components, and downstream app consumption
excluded.reason: These follow package creation and View editor entry.
Developer Center APIs assume an existing customer team rather than defining the Studio package first success.

sources.title: Getting started with the Celonis Platform
Inviting users to your Celonis Platform team
Creating packages
evidence_supported: View and Analysis Data Model requirement

sources.title: Celonis Free Plan Registration
evidence_supported: Client-rendered page without publicly readable required fields

sources.title: Creating applications and dashboards using Studio
evidence_supported: Studio depends on Data Models from Data Integration
Views, Analysis, Action Flow, and related asset types

sources.title: Get started with Celonis APIs
evidence_supported: API access assumes an existing Celonis customer team

uncertainties.question: Which Celonis task family and Studio initial-content choice define the platform-wide first success from account creation?
uncertainties.impact: Team admin, data integration, Studio View, Blank or Automation package, consumer Apps, and API routes change prerequisites, fields, waits, and the observable terminal.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A corpus decision that fixes one bounded developer goal, then a dated walkthrough proving that peer Studio initial-content options are longer or out of scope.

uncertainties.question: What exact Free Plan registration, invitation, or open-signup fields and post-join entitlements appear for a cold developer?
uncertainties.impact: Account-action and field counts cannot be verified, and Studio plus Data Model availability after join is unknown.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of signup.celonis.com or a fresh invitation or open-signup join, with screenshots of required fields and the resulting team license, roles, and Studio assets.

uncertainties.question: Can a no-account developer reach a View package terminal without a pre-existing Data Model or administrator-provisioned data?
uncertainties.impact: If not, the recorded Studio View first success is unreachable on the shortest cold path, and Blank, Automation, or a data-integration prefix may win.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Fresh Free Plan or trial evidence showing whether demo data or another zero-integration Data Model exists before Create package with View content.
